v2_sources.title: Zolbetuximab plus CAPOX in CLDN18.2-positive gastric or gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma: the randomized, phase 3 GLOW trial
v2_sources.citation: Zolbetuximab plus CAPOX in CLDN18.2-positive gastric or gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma: the randomized, phase 3 GLOW trial. Nature Medicine. 2023.

boundary_tags.population_or_model: CLDN18.2-positive gastric or gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma patients

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Primary endpoint PFS was met: median 8.21 months (zolbetuximab) versus 6.80 months (placebo); HR=0.687; 95% CI, 0.544–0.866; P=0.0007', 'page': 1}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Primary endpoint PFS was met: median 8.21 months (zolbetuximab) versus 6.80 months (placebo); HR=0.687; 95% CI, 0.544–0.866; P=0.0007', 'page': 1}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Key secondary endpoint OS was met: median 14.39 months (zolbetuximab) versus 12.16 months (placebo); HR=0.771; 95% CI, 0.615–0.965; P=0.0118', 'page': 1}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Key secondary endpoint OS was met: median 14.39 months (zolbetuximab) versus 12.16 months (placebo); HR=0.771; 95% CI, 0.615–0.965; P=0.0118', 'page': 1}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': '12-month OS rate: 58% vs 51%; 24-month OS rate: 29% vs 17%', 'page': 6}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': '12-month OS rate: 58% vs 51%; 24-month OS rate: 29% vs 17%', 'page': 6}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Objective response rate (ORR): 42.5% (zolbetuximab) versus 40.3% (placebo) in ITT population', 'page': 3}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Objective response rate (ORR): 42.5% (zolbetuximab) versus 40.3% (placebo) in ITT population', 'page': 3}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Median duration of response (DOR): 6.14 months (zolbetuximab) versus 6.08 months (placebo)', 'page': 3}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Median duration of response (DOR): 6.14 months (zolbetuximab) versus 6.08 months (placebo)', 'page': 3}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Most common TEAEs with zolbetuximab were nausea (68.5% vs 50.2%) and vomiting (66.1% vs 30.6%), mostly observed during first treatment cycle', 'page': 7}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Most common TEAEs with zolbetuximab were nausea (68.5% vs 50.2%) and vomiting (66.1% vs 30.6%), mostly observed during first treatment cycle', 'page': 7}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'In patients with measurable lesions, ORR was 53.8% (zolbetuximab) versus 48.8% (placebo)', 'page': 3}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'In patients with measurable lesions, ORR was 53.8% (zolbetuximab) versus 48.8% (placebo)', 'page': 3}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'OS benefit was consistent across most pre-specified subgroups including age, sex, region, prior gastrectomy status, and primary tumor site', 'page': 6}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'OS benefit was consistent across most pre-specified subgroups including age, sex, region, prior gastrectomy status, and primary tumor site', 'page': 6}
